div1.entryFree:
Honoured, adj. == honourable; 1) of high rank, noble: I am from humble, he from h. name, All's I, 3, 162. mingling them with us, the h. number, Cor. III, 1, 72. Applied to things relating to persons of quality: which then he wore upon his h. finger, Cymb. V, 5, 184 (cf. honourable: Ven. Ded. Cymb. V, 5, 184 Tim. I, 1, 97).
2) doing honour, reflecting credit on one: a custom more h. in the breach than the observance, Hml. I, 4, 16. kiss the h. gashes whole, Ant. IV, 8, 11.
3) illustrious: he comes to an h. triumph strangely furnished, Per. II, 2, 53.
4) consistent with honour, virtuous: in h. love, Lr. V, 1, 9.
